bidding.note: Clean negative X of 1H with 4 spades + 12 HCP; N/S reach making 3NT.
Light double (8 HCP); E/W buy it and declare 4H -- nothing to teach in N/S bidding.
The double is E's, not responder's; no negative double on display.
Negative X of 1H with 4 spades + 10 HCP; opener shows spades, raise to 2S makes.
2-level negative X of 2H with 4 spades + values; reaches making 3NT.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts + 11 HCP; 2H makes.
Responder bid 1S; the X is E's, S's XX is a support redouble -- no negative double.
Sound negative X but heavy later competition; E/W declare 3H -- N/S don't buy it.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts + 13 HCP; reaches making 3NT.
Negative X of 1S, then strength cue (2S) drives to making 3NT.
Sound negative X of 1S (4 hearts, 12 HCP); opener converts to penalty, 1SX-3 = +300.
Sound double but messy competition; E/W buy it at 3S -- nothing for N/S to learn.
Negative X of 2D should show BOTH majors; N is only 4-2, opener bids the doubleton spade -> 2S misfit -100.
Negative X of 1H with 4 spades + 5 diamonds; no major fit, lands in making 5C.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts + 10 HCP; 2H makes.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts; opener rebids own clubs (no heart fit), 2C makes.
2-level negative X of 2H with 4 spades; reaches making 3NT.
Negative X of 1H with 4 spades, slightly light (9 HCP); opener rebids 1NT, +120.
Light double (8 HCP); E/W compete to 3H and declare -- N/S don't buy it.
Responder bid 1S; S's X is a support double, not a responder negative double.
Negative X of 1H with 4 spades + 12 HCP; reaches making 3NT.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts; 2H makes with overtricks.
Responder bid 1S; S's X is a support double, not a responder negative double.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts + 10 HCP; 2H makes.
Sound double but heavy competition; E/W declare 4HX -- N/S are defending.
Classic negative X of 1D: 4-4 majors with a diamond void; reaches making 4C.
Negative X of 2D with 4-4 majors; strength cue drives to making 4H.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts; 2H makes with an overtrick.
Negative X is sound (4 hearts, 10 HCP); opener's penalty pass misfires, 1SX makes -160.
2-level negative X of 2H with 4 spades + values; reaches making 4S over 4H.
Responder bid 1H; S's X is a support double, not a responder negative double.
Light 4-4 double (8 HCP); E/W buy it and declare 3D -- N/S don't play it.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts + 12 HCP; 2H makes with overtricks.
Strong negative X of 2D (16 HCP, 4-4 majors); reaches making 4H.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts + 14 HCP; reaches making 4H.
Negative X of 1D with 4-4 majors; opener has no major (spade void), rebids 2C, +110.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts; reaches making 3NT through later competition.
Very light double (7 HCP); E/W balance into 2D and declare -- nothing for N/S.
6-HCP negative double is far too light; overreaches to a failing 4C, -50.
Responder bid 1H; S's X is a support double, not a responder negative double.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts; opener jumps to 3H, makes with overtricks.
Negative X of 1H with 4 spades; no spade fit, lands in making 2NT.
6-HCP double far too light; E/W declare 2H -- N/S don't buy it.
Strong negative X of 1D (14 HCP, both majors); reaches making 3NT.
Sound negative X of 1S (4 hearts); 25-HCP 3NT is normal but unluckily fails 2.
Negative X of 1S with 4 hearts; reaches making 3NT.
Negative X of 1D with 4-4 majors; finds heart fit, 3H makes.
Sound negative X of 2C with 4 spades; long cue-bid continuation overreaches to 5D-1.
Sound negative X of 1S (4 hearts, 13 HCP); opener converts to penalty, 1SX-4 = +800.
Sound negative X of 1H (4 spades); competitive 3C continuation fails by one.
Clean 1-level negative double of 1H (4 spades, 9 HCP); no spade fit so N/S settle in clubs.
Textbook negative double (4 spades), then competitive push to 3C over 2H.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts) is clean; 3NT is the wrinkle (DD fails), so the X is the teaching point not the contract.
Higher-level negative double of 2H: 4 spades, 13 HCP, void in their suit; N/S to a sound 3D.
Sound negative double (4 spades, 14 HCP); 3NT happens to fail at DD but the X is correct.
Negative double of 2D is fine, but E's 3D buys the contract and declares (NS -110); nothing to teach in N/S play of the final spot.
Classic negative double (4 spades, 13 HCP) finds the 4-4 spade fit; 4S makes.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts) opposite a strong 1D; N/S reach a making 3NT.
The X here is opener's support double, not responder's negative double; off-scheme for this lesson.
Negative double of 1D shows both majors (5-4); N/S reach a making 3NT.
Negative double of 1H with 4 spades but only 8 HCP — fine at the 1 level; N/S settle in 3C.
Negative double (4 spades) is sound; the close part is N/S competing to 4D down one in a live auction.
Negative double of 1S (4 spades... 4 hearts), then a balancing reopening sequence to 3D.
Negative double of 1D shows both majors (4-4); N/S find the 4-4 spade fit, 4S makes.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts); no major fit, N/S rest in 2D.
Negative double (4 spades) is clean; 3NT happens to fail at DD.
The X is by the 4th-seat opponent over N/S's 1C-1D-1H, not a responder negative double; off-scheme.
Higher-level negative double of 2H (4 spades, 14 HCP); N/S find spades and bid an excellent 6S.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades); no fit, N/S rest in 2NT.
Negative double of 1D shows both majors (4-4) with only 8 HCP; N/S play 1H.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades, void in hearts); competitive sequence to 4D.
Negative double of 1D with 5-4 majors but only 6 HCP — light; some would pass and await reopening.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts); N/S play 2H making.
Higher-level negative double of 2H (4 spades); no fit, N/S rest in 3D.
The X is opener's support double after 1D-P-1H-2C, not a responder negative double; off-scheme.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts); N/S find the heart fit and reach 4H making.
Negative double of 1D (5-4 majors); N/S reach 3H (fails at DD).
Negative double of 1D (both majors 4-4) sound; heavy later competition ends in 3CX (a teaching wrinkle, not the X itself).
Negative double of 1D (both majors); N/S find spades, 4S makes.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts); no fit, N/S settle in 2H (DD light).
Higher-level negative double of 2D (both majors); N/S play 2S making (19 HCP opposite).
Negative double of 1D is fine but W jumps to 4D and buys it (NS -150); E/W declare, nothing to teach in N/S.
Higher-level negative double of 2H (4 spades, 16 HCP); long sound game auction to 5D.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades) sound; the 3H jump and later 5H sacrifice are the live-auction wrinkle.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades, 7 HCP — light but fine at 1 level); E/W push to 3H.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts) sound, but E/W buy 4S and declare (NS +100 on defense); nothing to teach in N/S play.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts) sound; over the 3S raise N/S compete to a making 5D.
Negative double of 2H (4 spades) is fine but E buys 3H and declares (NS +100 on defense); N/S do not play.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades) fine, but E/W reopen and buy 2H (NS -110); E/W declare, nothing to teach in N/S.
Negative double of 1D (5-4 majors, 14 HCP); N/S cue-bid their way to 4S making.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades); no fit, N/S reach 3NT making.
Negative double of 1D (both majors) fine, but E/W buy 4D and declare (NS -130); nothing to teach in N/S.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades) fine, but E/W reopen and buy 2H (NS -140); E/W declare.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts, 14 HCP); N/S reach 3NT making.
Negative double of 1S (4 hearts) fine, but E/W buy 3S and declare (NS -140); nothing to teach in N/S.
Negative double of 1S with 6-4 (spades behind the overcaller, 4 hearts) — awkward shape but X-then-bid works; N/S reach 6H.
The X is opener's support double after 1C-P-1H-2D, not a responder negative double; off-scheme.
Negative double of 1H (4 spades); no fit, N/S rest in 2NT.
Neg X of 2H shows 4 spades + values; N has 4S 13hcp, N/S reach making 3N.
Neg X of 1D shows both majors; N is 4-5 majors, cue then 4S making +2.
Double is sound (4S, 14hcp) but N/S land in 4S which fails double-dummy (down_both).
Responder bids 1S naturally; no negative double on display, and E/W declare 3D.
Sound 4S/heart double but N/S drift into 4C, which fails double-dummy.
Clean neg X (4S, 14hcp); E's jump to 3H is extra pressure, N's 3N is sound and makes.
Neg X of 1S shows 4 hearts; N has 4H, N/S reach 4H making +1.
Neg X of 1H shows 4 spades; spade fit found, 4S makes.
E raises to 2D and N/S are shut out; E/W declare 2D, nothing to teach in N/S.
Responder bids 1H naturally; the X is opener's support double, not a responder negative double.
Light 4-card-spade double; N/S outbid and E/W declare 3H, no N/S contract to teach.
Light 2-level double (4S, 9hcp); S jumps to 4C which fails double-dummy.
Responder bids 1H naturally; the X is opener's support double, not a responder negative double.
Neg X of 1H shows 4 spades; N has 4S 14hcp, N/S reach 3N making +2.
Neg X of 2D; N is only 4-3 in the majors but values are fine, 4H makes.
Responder bids 1S naturally; the X is opener's support double, not a responder negative double.
Clean neg X (4S, 14hcp); long cue-bid sequence to 4S making +1.
W passed the opening, so responder's 1S is uncontested; the X is opener's support double, not a negative double.
Neg X of 1H shows 4 spades; N has 4S 18hcp, N/S reach 3N making +2.
Sound 5-4 majors double, but N/S land in 3N that fails double-dummy.
N/S outbid and E/W declare 3D; no N/S contract to teach.
Neg X of 1S shows 4 hearts; opener bids 2H, passed out making +2.
Clean 4-4 majors double, but N/S land in 2N which fails double-dummy.
Light double (4 weak spades, 8hcp); N/S rest in 2C making +1.
Responder bids 1S naturally; the X is opener's support double, not a negative double.
Neg X of 2C is passed for penalty; E/W play 2C-doubled and make it (NS -180).
Neg X of 1S shows 4 hearts; N has 4H, N/S reach 3N making +1.
Sound 4-heart double but E/W declare 2S (set); no N/S contract to teach.
Responder bids 1H naturally; the X is opener's support double, not a negative double.
Neg X of 1H shows 4 spades; N has 4S 14hcp, N/S reach 3N making +1.
Sound 4-spade double; competitive auction settles in 3C making +1.
5-4 majors with a club void; N passes opener's 1N rather than pulling, lands 1N making +1.
Neg X of 1H (4S, 16hcp); spade fit found but N/S stop in 2S (slight underbid), making +2.
Clean 5-4 majors double; long cue-bid sequence to 4H making +2.
Neg X of 1S is passed out for penalty; E/W make 1S-doubled (NS -160).
Responder bids 1S naturally (limit-raise X is E's); not a responder negative double, and 4S fails double-dummy.
Neg X of 1H (4S + 6 clubs); no spade fit, N/S settle in 3N making.
Neg X of 1S shows 4 hearts; N has 4H, S has 16hcp, N/S reach 3N making.
Sound 4-heart double; close NT invite to 2N (DD-makeable) goes down a trick at the table.
Very light double (4S, 6hcp); S jumps to 3N which fails double-dummy.
Sound 4-heart double but E/W declare 3S (set); no N/S contract to teach.
Neg X of 2H shows spades; N has 4S 10hcp, opener raises to 3S making.
Very light double (4-4 majors, 6hcp); N/S outbid and E/W declare 3D.
Very light double (4S, 6hcp); S jumps to 2S which fails double-dummy.
Sound 4-spade double but N/S drift into 4C which fails double-dummy.
Neg X of 1H shows 4 spades; no spade fit, N/S rest in 2D making +2.
